item.title: About the Group
item.description: Who we are, where we meet, and what young people get from us.

item.title: What does a typical meeting look like?
item.description:
Meetings run from arrival through investiture-style opening to a mix of games, badge work and outdoor activity, then a closing circle. Squirrels and Beavers run shorter sessions; Cubs, Scouts and Explorers run longer. Specific timings per section are on the cards above.

Most weeks we’re indoors at the hut, but we head outside whenever the weather and programme allow. Patrol/Six leaders run smaller groups within the meeting.

item.title: Do I have to volunteer to get my child a place?
item.description:
No — but it helps. We have a long-standing principle that families who join in get priority on the waiting list. There’s a role for every amount of time you can give, from washing-up after camp to running a section. See the volunteer page for what we need.

Many parents volunteer for one or two terms while their child is settling in. There’s no expectation it becomes a long-term commitment unless you want it to.

item.title: What each section does
item.description: A quick read on each age group, what they do, and how they progress.

item.title: Can my child come to a try-it session first?
item.description:
Yes — every section runs a few trial weeks at the start of each term. Once you’re on the waiting list, the section leader will be in touch with try-it dates as places come up.

We don’t run open evenings as a rule — section meetings are busy and better suited to one child at a time joining in for a few weeks before formally enrolling.

item.title: How to join
item.description: Waiting list, subs, what to bring on the first night.

item.title: Safeguarding & photo policy
item.description: How we keep young people safe and how we handle photos.

item.title: The year at a glance
item.description: Termly rhythms and the fixtures every family should know about.

item.title: District Rally
item.description:
The District Rally has been rescheduled from October to late November to align with the District’s outdoor programme. Same venue, new date — full Group day with backwoods cooking, pioneering, a wide game in the afternoon, and the District flag ceremony to finish.

If you subscribed to our events calendar you don’t need to do anything; the meeting will update automatically.

Bring a packed lunch and a warm layer.

item.title: Leaders' Safeguarding Refresher
item.description:
This event has been cancelled. The District training calendar is being revised and we’ll publish a new date once the new programme is confirmed — keep an eye on the events page.

Volunteers who had this in their calendar via our subscription feed will see the meeting disappear automatically.

item.title: Cubs Summer Camp
item.description:
Three days under canvas at Pioneer Camp — Friday afternoon arrival, Sunday afternoon collection. The Pack will be running a mix of campcraft, water activities and the all-time-favourite night hike.

The kit list for this camp is linked below; consent forms come home four weeks ahead. Bursary support is available — speak to Akela in confidence if cost would otherwise stop your Cub joining us.

item.title: Summer Fete
item.description:
Our annual Summer Fete on the Hut grounds — bouncy castle, BBQ, raffle, plus a chance for parents to meet the leadership team and learn about the year ahead.

Volunteers welcome — get in touch if you can help with the BBQ or the gates rota.

item.title: Brussels District planning meeting
item.description:
A short planning call for Section Leaders and Assistants across the Brussels District ahead of the autumn term. We’ll cover the term card, the District camp shortlist, safeguarding refresher dates, and any help needed on the volunteer rota.

Dial-in details will be sent by email the day before. If you can’t make it, drop the GSL a note and we’ll fold your input into the notes.

item.title: Assistant Cub Leader
item.description: Help run our Wednesday Cub Pack: games, badge work, the occasional bit of controlled chaos, and the camps that Cubs remember for years. You’d work alongside Akela and the rest of the team — no experience needed, just reliability and a sense of humour. We’ll fund and book your training, and you’ll never be on your own with the Pack.

item.title: Fundraising Lead
item.description: Coordinate the Group’s fundraising calendar — Christmas Post, the 100 Club, and the seasonal events. (This role is currently filled; the front-matter published = false keeps it off the live listing.)

item.title: Group Treasurer
item.description: Keep the Group’s finances healthy: pay the bills, bank the takings, keep the books, and present the accounts at the AGM. It’s a Trustee role, so it carries real responsibility — but it’s very doable remotely, which makes it a great fit for a UK-based parent or supporter who can’t get to weekly meetings. Bookkeeping or finance experience is welcome but not essential; the outgoing Treasurer will hand over thoroughly.

item.title: Photo consent
item.description:
This page is the starter wording shipped with the British Scout Group theme. Replace the body below with the policy your Group has agreed with its Trustees. Nothing on this page is rendered as a legal instrument until you’ve reviewed it with your safeguarding lead.

Why we ask

Some of what we do is naturally photogenic — camps, badge presentations, parades. Sharing photos helps families see what their child has been up to and shows prospective members what life in the Group is like. We also have a duty of care to the young people in those photos.

item.title: Trustee (Group Executive)
item.description: Our Trustee Board (the Group Executive) looks after the Group’s money, property, safeguarding duties and overall direction. We’re looking for people with skills in finance, fundraising, premises, HR or law — but also just sensible people who care about local young people. Meetings can be joined online, so this works well for supporters living away from the area, including back in the UK.

item.title: St George's Day Parade
item.description: The whole Group turned out for our annual St George’s Day Parade — fresh neckers, polished shoes, and a piper to lead us in.

item.title: Cubs Cooking Cup 2026
item.description: Six teams. Three courses. One champion. Notes from the most-contested Cooking Cup the Pack has ever seen.

item.title: Squirrels start in September
item.description: We’re delighted to announce that 1st Anytown will open a Squirrel Drey from September. Details on joining and how to express interest below.

item.title: Beavers — Summer Term 2026
item.description: A bright, gentle Summer term for Beavers — finishing with a Colony sleepover at the hut.

item.title: Cubs — Summer Term 2026
item.description: This term we’re building towards the Naturalist activity badge and a weekend Pack camp at Anytown Common. A typical Cubs term: fire-lighting, map work, a community visit, and plenty of outdoor time. Half-term falls in week 8 — no meeting that week.

item.title: Cubs Summer Camp Kit List (desert climate)
Cubs Weekend Camp Kit List (desert climate)
Governance
item.description: Every parent and carer of a member is welcome at the AGM, where the Trustee Board reports on the year, presents the accounts, and stands for re-election. We’d genuinely welcome more hands on the board — particularly anyone with finance, fundraising or premises experience. If that might be you, have a word with any of the trustees listed above.

item.title: Home range and InTouch
item.description: This page is gated by params.bso.show_home_range in your site’s hugo.toml. Default is false — most BSO Groups don’t need a formal home-range publication. Flip the param to true to surface the link in the /bso/ hub.

item.title: Map check at the second checkpoint
Moving to the United Arab Emirates
Our history
item.description: We started in 1974 with six children and two borrowed trestle tables. A pair of parents who had run Scout sections back in the UK found themselves overseas, far from the nearest Group, and decided their own children — and their friends — shouldn’t miss out. The first meetings ran in a villa majlis; within a few years we had affiliated to British Scouting Overseas and were running the same programme, and working towards the same awards, as Groups at home.

item.title: Scouting in the United Arab Emirates
item.description: The data behind this page lives in data/bso/alternatives/ae.toml — your Group edits this file to add or correct entries. The theme ships a starter dataset for the eight most-common BSO host countries: NL, BE, FR, DE, ES, AE, SG, US.

item.title: Scouts — Autumn Term 2025
item.description: Last autumn’s Scouts programme — kept here for District audit and as a demonstration of the archive view (set params.programme.keep_archive true to see it in the listing).

item.title: Scouts — Autumn Term 2026
item.description: A look ahead to next autumn — published early so parents can plan around the weekend hike and Remembrance parade.

item.title: Scouts Summer Camp Kit List (desert climate)
Scouts Weekend Camp Kit List (desert climate)
Setting off as the light fades
Squirrels and Beavers on parade outside the church
